text: Previous history of stress fracture is the strongest risk factor for future lower extremity stress fracture in runners, with nearly 5-fold increased odds.
Female sex is a significant risk factor for lower extremity stress fracture in runners, with approximately 2.3-fold increased odds compared to males.
Stress fractures account for 15-20% of all musculoskeletal injuries among runners.
Up to 95% of stress fractures occur in the lower extremities.
Runners with a previous stress fracture should be monitored with heightened vigilance for recurrence, as prior history is the single strongest predictor of future stress fracture.
